arguments.task: Research Higgsfield using official first-party pages only where possible. Starting from https://higgsfield.ai/cli, find and extract: 1) whether Higgsfield offers public developer/API/CLI documentation, 2) authentication method(s) described for CLI or API, 3) pricing or access gating for API/CLI, 4) whether there is a public API and its type (REST/GraphQL/none), 5) any rate limit notes, 6) any official mention of MCP/server/agent tooling, 7) collect exact source URLs for each claim. Prefer official docs, pricing, support, changelog, GitHub, or MCP registry pages. Return a concise structured summary with URLs and quote snippets when possible.

input.thought: Launch a browser task to inspect official Higgsfield pages and extract API, auth, access, and MCP evidence.

raw_result.description: AI video/image generation platform with a CLI, MCP endpoint, and agent-oriented Skills integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official CLI/MCP docs show interactive `higgsfield auth login`; MCP page provides a remote MCP server URL for Claude/ChatGPT/Cursor. The exact underlying credential format/token flow is not fully verified from gathered evidence, so auth classification is partially inferred from product login behavior and needs review.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing page is publicly accessible and CLI/MCP pages are public. Pricing notes indicate CLI/MCP access is available to regular users but uses credits rather than free web generations; no contact-sales or partner gate was verified.

api_surface.breadth: Public evidence gathered shows a CLI, a hosted MCP endpoint, and Skills integration pages, but no clearly documented public REST or GraphQL developer API was verified from the gathered sources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP page exposes a hosted MCP server URL (`https://mcp.higgsfield.ai/mcp`) and setup guidance for Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor; GitHub CLI README also mentions MCP Registry.

raw_result.blocker: No clearly documented public REST/GraphQL API was verified; toolkit build would likely need to rely on the hosted MCP server and/or CLI rather than a conventional API.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No explicit rate-limit documentation was verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official site has dedicated CLI, MCP, and Skills pages; official GitHub repo exists for the CLI; pricing page explicitly notes that unlimited models and free generations on higgsfield.ai are not accessible on MCP/CLI, Canvas, or Supercomputer, implying CLI/MCP usage is metered by credits.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Uncertainty remains on exact auth method classification and whether Higgsfield has any separate documented REST/GraphQL API beyond CLI/MCP. Access tier may also depend on credits/paid usage for meaningful automation even though pages are self-serve public.
